mentions.note: Developing Trainium and Trainium alternatives to reduce Nvidia chip reliance. If equity offering follows Google's, will dilute supply further just before SpaceX IPO.
May do a fundraiser per Cramer's speculation. Supply could hit stock if they tap capital markets.
Heavy data center capex similar to Microsoft; may need to raise equity to stay competitive. Not yet public on plans but could be forced to tap markets alongside Alphabet, Anthropic, SpaceX trio.

Notably absent from Jensen's client shout-outs despite owning Anthropic stake ($14B). Cramer notes competitive tension; in-house chip development challenges Jensen's stance but Amazon not defending position.

Misunderstood but now huge believer; cleaning up with Prime, AWS, advertising, and semiconductors (own chips $50B standalone business potential); new chips hold value similar to Nvidia but lower price; reason Nvidia stalled while Amazon climbed.
Hyperscale data center operator and AI cloud player; Nvidia reported 115% hyperscale revenue growth; Amazon positioned to benefit from infrastructure buildout.
FANG stock and Magnificent Seven member; borrowed heavily in early days but massive opportunity; secular growth with scalability.
High-quality growth name that performed well during recession; example of company that works in thick or thin economic cycles.

Andy Jassy noted in shareholder letter that Amazon actually lowered prices vs. last year; valuable FedEx customer (recent relationship); competing 3PL offering is non-asset play, not threat to FedEx true end-to-end network; win-win relationship.
Cloud/data center provider in infrastructure layer. Spending hundreds of billions on AI data center expansion and jobs.
AWS data center capex commitment is existential moat. CEO Andy Jassy: not spending enough means losing billion-dollar customers to Alphabet/Microsoft. Data center packed with paying customers.
Spending tens of billions now for hundreds of billions later. Data center capex ensures colossal multi-year profits. One of two main publicly traded winners in cloud race.
Ultimate trade-down play; computer-driven economy immunity to oil/rate shocks; dominates new economy with logistics, cloud, and supply chain services.
$200B AI capex investment transformational; supply chain, semiconductors, and satellites create new $15B+ revenue streams; prices falling despite inflation through operational excellence.
